name: microsoft-teamsserver-mcp-foundry
title: Work IQ Teams MCP Server
summary: Microsoft Teams Graph MCP server to manage chats, channels and users or messages within them....
description: Microsoft Teams Graph MCP server to manage chats, channels and users or messages within them. Use for Teams operations like creating, updating, retrieval of chats and chat messages, managing teams and channels.

useCases.name: ListTeams
useCases.description: List Teams workspaces the signed-in user is a member of. Returns each team's id (GUID), displayName, description, and webUrl. Use as a prerequisite before channel operations.

useCases.name: ListChats
useCases.description: Get the signed-in user's recent Teams chats, or filter by topic or member UPNs. Without filters, returns recent chats with hasUnreadMessages and lastMessagePreview — use for 'catch me up' or 'any new messages'. With filters, returns matching chats. To message a specific person, prefer SendMessageToUser (pass their UPN directly).

useCases.name: ListChannels
useCases.description: Lists all channels in a Microsoft Teams team. Returns each channel's id, displayName, description, membershipType (standard/private/shared), createdDateTime, and webUrl. Prerequisite: Call ListTeams first to obtain a valid teamId GUID — this tool requires a system-retrieved teamId and cannot accept team names or fabricated IDs.

useCases.name: GetTeam
useCases.description: Get detailed information about a specific Microsoft Teams workspace. Returns the team's id, displayName, description, createdDateTime, and webUrl. Prerequisite: Call ListTeams first to obtain a valid teamId GUID — this tool requires a system-retrieved teamId and cannot accept team names or fabricated IDs.

useCases.name: GetChat
useCases.description: Get details of a specific Teams chat by ID. Returns the chat's id, topic, chatType (oneOnOne/group), createdDateTime, and lastUpdatedDateTime. Prerequisite: Obtain chatId from ListChats if unknown.

useCases.name: CreateChat
useCases.description: Create or retrieve a chat. To send a message to a person, prefer SendMessageToUser — it is simpler and handles chat creation internally. Use CreateChat for group chats (pass multiple UPNs and optionally a topic) or when you only need the chatId without sending a message. For oneOnOne: pass the other person's UPN in members_upns — you are auto-added, and if the chat already exists it is returned (no duplicate). Returns the chat's id, chatType, topic, and createdDateTime.

useCases.name: DeleteChat
useCases.description: Delete a Teams chat by ID. This action is irreversible. Returns confirmation with the chatId. Prerequisite: Obtain chatId from ListChats if unknown.

useCases.name: UpdateChat
useCases.description: Update a group chat's topic/display name. Only works on group chats — oneOnOne chats cannot have topics. Returns the updated chat's id and topic. Prerequisite: Obtain chatId from ListChats if unknown.

useCases.name: GetChannel
useCases.description: Get details of a specific channel within a team. Returns the channel's id, displayName, description, membershipType, createdDateTime, and webUrl. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams then ListChannels to obtain required teamId and channelId.

useCases.name: CreateChannel
useCases.description: Create a new channel in a Teams team. Use membershipType to control access: 'standard' (default, visible to all team members), 'private' (restricted to invited members — calling user is set as owner), or 'shared' (cross-team collaboration — calling user is set as owner). Returns the channel's id, displayName, membershipType, and createdDateTime. Prerequisite: Call ListTeams first to obtain the teamId GUID — do not pass team names.

useCases.name: UpdateChannel
useCases.description: Update a channel's display name, description, or both. Returns the updated channel's id, displayName, and description. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams then ListChannels to obtain required IDs.

useCases.name: ListChatMembers
useCases.description: List all members of a Teams chat. Returns each member's id, displayName, email, userId, and roles (owner/guest). Prerequisite: Obtain chatId from ListChats if unknown.

useCases.name: ListChannelMembers
useCases.description: List all members of a Teams channel. Returns each member's id, displayName, email, userId, and roles. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams then ListChannels to obtain required teamId and channelId.

useCases.name: AddChatMember
useCases.description: Add a member to an existing Teams chat. Returns confirmation with the member's id and chatId. Prerequisite: Obtain chatId from ListChats and resolve the user via a lookup tool.

useCases.name: AddChannelMember
useCases.description: Add a member to a Teams channel. Supports 'owner' (default) or 'member' role. Standard channels inherit team membership and do not support adding members directly. Private and shared channels support both roles. Returns confirmation with the member and channel details. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams then ListChannels for the IDs, and resolve the userId via a user lookup tool.

useCases.name: UpdateChannelMember
useCases.description: Update a channel member's role to 'owner' or 'member'. Returns confirmation with the updated member details. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams, ListChannels, and ListChannelMembers to obtain required IDs.

useCases.name: SendFileToChat
useCases.description: Send a file to a Teams chat by chatId. Best for group chats or when you already have the chatId. To send a file to a specific person by UPN/email, prefer SendFileToUser instead — it is simpler and does not require a chatId. Provide either fileUrl (existing SharePoint/OneDrive file) or fileContentBase64 + fileName (upload and send). Max 4 MB for direct upload. Uploaded files are stored in the sender's OneDrive and shared with an organization-scoped link (accessible only to people in the same tenant). An optional text message can accompany the file. REQUIRED CHAIN: If chatId is unknown, call ListChats first. DO NOT fabricate chatIds.

useCases.name: SendFileToUser
useCases.description: Send a file directly to a specific user by their UPN (email) or GUID — no need to call ListChats or CreateChat first. The 1:1 chat is created or retrieved automatically (idempotent). Provide either fileUrl (existing SharePoint/OneDrive file) or fileContentBase64 + fileName (upload and send). Max 4 MB for direct upload. Uploaded files are stored in the sender's OneDrive and shared with an organization-scoped link (accessible only to people in the same tenant). An optional text message can accompany the file. Prefer this for sending files to a person; use SendFileToChat when you already have a chatId.

useCases.name: SendFileToChannel
useCases.description: Send a file to a Teams channel. Provide either fileUrl (existing SharePoint/OneDrive file) or fileContentBase64 + fileName (upload to channel folder and send). Max 4 MB for direct upload. Uploaded files are stored in the channel's SharePoint folder (or the sender's OneDrive as fallback) and shared with an organization-scoped link (accessible only to people in the same tenant). An optional text message can accompany the file. REQUIRED CHAIN: ListTeams -> ListChannels -> this tool.

useCases.name: ListChannelFiles
useCases.description: List files shared in a Teams channel. Returns each file's name, size, webUrl, lastModifiedDateTime, and lastModifiedBy. PAGING: When the response contains hasMoreResults: true, call this tool again passing the nextLink value as the nextLink parameter to retrieve the next page. REQUIRED CHAIN: ListTeams -> ListChannels -> this tool.

useCases.name: SendMessageToUser
useCases.description: Send a message directly to a specific user by their UPN (email) or GUID — no need to call CreateChat first. The chat is created or retrieved automatically (idempotent — no duplicate). Supports plain text, HTML formatting, @mentions, Adaptive Cards, and importance. Call GetRichMessageFormats for formatting options. Returns the sent message's id, chatId, and createdDateTime. Prefer this for direct 1:1 messages; use SendMessageToChat when you already have a chatId.

useCases.name: ListChatMessages
useCases.description: List messages in a Teams chat. Returns messages ordered by most recent first, each containing: id, createdDateTime, sender (displayName, userId), and body (contentType, content). PAGING: When the response contains hasMoreResults: true, call this tool again passing the nextLink value as the nextLink parameter to retrieve the next page. Prerequisite: If the chatId is unknown, call ListChats first to find the target chat.

useCases.name: ListChannelMessages
useCases.description: List root messages in a Teams channel (without replies). Returns messages ordered by most recent first, each containing: id, timestamps, sender (displayName, userId), and body content. PAGING: When the response contains hasMoreResults: true, call this tool again passing the nextLink value as the nextLink parameter to retrieve the next page. To read a specific thread's replies, use ListChannelMessageReplies. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams then ListChannels to obtain required IDs.

useCases.name: SendMessageToChat
useCases.description: Send a message to an existing Teams chat by chatId. Best for group chats, replying in an ongoing conversation, or when you already have the chatId. To message a specific person by UPN/email, prefer SendMessageToUser instead — it is simpler and does not require a chatId. Supports plain text, HTML formatting, @mentions, Adaptive Cards, and importance. Call GetRichMessageFormats for formatting options.

useCases.name: SendMessageToSelf
useCases.description: Send a note or reminder to yourself in Teams (the 'Notes to Self' chat). No chatId needed. Supports plain text, HTML, @mentions, Adaptive Cards, and importance. Call GetRichMessageFormats for formatting options. Prefer this over SendMessageToChat for self-directed messages.

useCases.name: SendMessageToChannel
useCases.description: Send a message to a team channel. Supports plain text, HTML formatting, @mentions (users/teams/channels/apps via mentions param), Adaptive Cards, and message importance. Call GetRichMessageFormats for formatting options and templates. Returns the message id, teamId, channelId, and createdDateTime. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams then ListChannels to obtain required IDs.

useCases.name: GetChatMessage
useCases.description: Get a specific message from a Teams chat by ID. Returns the message's id, timestamps, sender (displayName, userId), and body (contentType, content). Prerequisites: Obtain chatId from ListChats and messageId from ListChatMessages.

useCases.name: UpdateChatMessage
useCases.description: Update the content of a message in a Teams chat. Supports plain text (default) or HTML. Only the message sender can update their own messages. Returns confirmation with the message id. Prerequisites: Obtain chatId from ListChats and messageId from ListChatMessages.

useCases.name: DeleteChatMessage
useCases.description: Delete a message from a Teams chat. This action is irreversible. Returns confirmation with chatId and messageId. Prerequisites: Obtain chatId from ListChats and messageId from ListChatMessages.

useCases.name: ReplyToChannelMessage
useCases.description: Reply to a specific message in a Teams channel (creates a threaded reply). Supports plain text, HTML formatting, @mentions, Adaptive Cards, and importance. Returns the reply's id, parentMessageId, and createdDateTime. For rich formatting, call GetRichMessageFormats first — it has HTML tags, mention syntax, and card examples. Prerequisites: Call ListTeams, ListChannels, and ListChannelMessages to obtain required IDs.

useCases.name: ListChannelMessageReplies
useCases.description: Get replies to a specific channel message thread. Returns each reply's id, sender (displayName, userId), body, and timestamp. PAGING: When the response contains hasMoreResults: true, call this tool again passing the nextLink value as the nextLink parameter to retrieve the next page. Use after ListChannelMessages to read a specific thread. Prerequisites: Obtain teamId, channelId, and messageId from prior tool calls.

useCases.name: GetRichMessageFormats
useCases.description: Reference guide for composing rich Teams messages. Returns supported HTML tags, @mention syntax for users/teams/channels/apps, Adaptive Card templates, and importance levels. Call before composing formatted messages.

useCases.name: GetUserPresence
useCases.description: Get the real-time Teams presence of a single user — i.e. whether they are currently available, busy, in a meeting, away, etc. Returns two distinct fields: (1) availability — the high-level state (Available, AvailableIdle, Away, BeRightBack, Busy, BusyIdle, DoNotDisturb, Offline, PresenceUnknown); (2) activity — what they are doing right now (Available, Away, BeRightBack, Busy, DoNotDisturb, InACall, InAConferenceCall, Inactive, InAMeeting, Offline, OffWork, OutOfOffice, Presenting, UrgentInterruptionsOnly, PresenceUnknown). Use this tool to answer questions like 'is John online', 'is Sarah in a meeting right now', 'who's available', 'should I ping X now or wait'. If the caller is about to send a Teams message, call this FIRST so you can advise whether it's a good time — then proceed to SendMessageToUser. Do NOT use this tool to find time for a future meeting; use FindMeetingTimes (calendar server) for scheduling. The user parameter accepts a UPN ('user@contoso.com'), an Entra object ID (GUID), OR a display name ('Jane Doe', 'demouser') — the tool will resolve the identifier internally. If a display name is ambiguous (multiple matches), the tool returns an error asking for a more specific value or a UPN. Omit the user parameter to return the signed-in user's own presence.

useCases.name: SearchTeamsMessagesUpdatePRTest
useCases.description: Search Teams message content using natural language. Use for broad or exploratory queries (e.g., 'messages about the deploy', 'what did Sarah say about the budget', 'discussions from last week'). Returns a summary of matching messages, a conversationId for follow-up queries, and chatIds that can be used with ListChatMessages to read full history. For precise keyword/sender/date searches, prefer SearchTeamMessagesQueryParameters — it is faster and more precise. To find chats by name or member, use ListChats.

useCases.name: SearchTeamMessagesQueryParameters
useCases.description: Search Teams messages using structured KQL (Keyword Query Language) queries. Best for precise searches with exact keywords, sender names/emails, or date ranges (e.g., 'from:jane@contoso.com AND budget', 'sent>=2025-01-01'). Searches across 1:1 chats and channel messages. Max 25 results per request. For natural language queries where exact terms are unknown, use SearchTeamsMessages instead.

mcpTeamsServerOAuth2.description: Authenticate with Work IQ using OAuth2 authorization code flow with PKCE support.
